text: Design is not just what it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works.
author: Steve Jobs
context: This quote fundamentally shaped my understanding of user experience design during my early career at tech startups. It taught me that beautiful interfaces mean nothing without thoughtful functionality.

text: The best way to predict the future is to create it.
author: Peter Drucker
context: A constant reminder that drives my entrepreneurial spirit and product thinking. Instead of waiting for change, I learned to be the architect of innovation.

author: Leonardo da Vinci
context: My guiding principle for creating clean, intuitive user interfaces. Every design decision I make is filtered through this lens of elegant simplicity.

text: Innovation distinguishes between a leader and a follower.
author: Steve Jobs
context: This mindset helped me transition from corporate design to startup innovation. It pushed me to always question the status quo and find better solutions.

author: Saul Bass
context: The foundation of my design philosophy - every visual choice should have reasoning. This quote reminds me that design is problem-solving, not decoration.

author: Louis Sullivan
source: The Tall Office Building Artistically Considered
context: A timeless principle that guides my approach to product design and architecture. Beauty emerges naturally when function is prioritized.

text: Good design is obvious. Great design is transparent.
author: Joe Sparano
context: This taught me that the best user experiences are invisible - users accomplish their goals without thinking about the interface.

text: Design creates culture. Culture shapes values. Values determine the future.
author: Robert L. Peters
context: Understanding the broader impact of design decisions on society and user behavior. Design isn't neutral - it shapes how people think and act.
